summary: SVG text elements lose their text content and x/y position attributes when multiple SKSvgCanvas instances are used in parallel via Task.Run on Windows; Linux is unaffected and the bug is a regression from SkiaSharp 2.88.9.

stepsToReproduce: Create multiple Task.Run calls that each create an SKSvgCanvas writing to a MemoryStream
In each task, draw multiple text elements using canvas.DrawText with SKFont and SKPaint
After canvas disposal, decode the SVG bytes to string
Check for blank <text> elements using regex: >\s+</text>

repoLinks.description: Full reproduction project with detailed README

bugSignals.errorMessage: SVG <text> elements rendered with empty text content and empty x/y attributes when SKSvgCanvas is used in parallel

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The SVG text rendering path and DirectWrite glyph-to-unicode mapping code have not changed since 3.116.0. The bug affects all 3.x versions.

regression.reason: Reporter confirms 2.88.9 does not have the bug. The text rendering pipeline was restructured in 3.x to use SKTextBlob and GlyphRunList, which changed how SVG text is emitted. The glyph-to-unicode reverse mapping via DirectWrite's GetGlyphIndices is the likely race condition point.

analysis.summary: Race condition in Skia's SVG text rendering path on Windows. When multiple SKSvgCanvas instances draw text in parallel, the SVGTextBuilder's glyph-to-unicode conversion via DirectWrite produces empty results intermittently. Each task uses separate canvas/stream/paint/font objects, so this is not a user-side threading violation — it's an internal thread-safety issue in the DirectWrite text shaping pipeline that the SVG device's onDrawGlyphRunList depends on.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly a bug: the reporter uses separate SkiaSharp objects per task (no sharing), yet gets corrupted output. The empty x/y attributes and empty text content in the SVG output indicate that SVGTextBuilder received zero-length glyph runs or GlyphsToUnichars returned all-zero unicodes. Windows-only behavior points to DirectWrite (Windows font backend) vs FreeType (Linux). The 2.88.9 → 3.x regression aligns with the text pipeline restructuring. Labels are already correct.

keySignals.text: text elements will sometimes have their text value and x/y attribute values removed
keySignals.interpretation: SVGTextBuilder produces empty fText, fPosXStr, fPosYStr — all three strings from glyph processing are empty, suggesting glyphs-to-unichars returned all zeros or runSize was 0.

keySignals.text: The more text elements being drawn and the more threads running the same task, the more likely the problem is to occur
keySignals.interpretation: Classic race condition scaling behavior — probability increases with contention.

keySignals.text: This affects all SkiaSharp 3.x versions on Windows. Linux is unaffected.
keySignals.interpretation: Windows uses DirectWrite for font operations; Linux uses FreeType. DirectWrite's IDWriteFontFace::GetGlyphIndices may have internal state that is not thread-safe when called concurrently.

keySignals.text: This is a regression from SkiaSharp 2.88.9 which does not have the bug.
keySignals.interpretation: The 3.x text pipeline routes through SKTextBlob → GlyphRunList → SVGDevice::onDrawGlyphRunList → GlyphsToUnichars → DirectWrite, a different path than 2.88.x.

codeInvestigation.finding: SVGTextBuilder converts glyph IDs back to unicode chars via SkFontPriv::GlyphsToUnichars. If glyphs map to zero (\0), they are discarded (line 1068-1072), resulting in empty text and no position strings. This matches the observed symptom of empty x/y attributes and empty text content.
GlyphsToUnichars calls typeface->getGlyphToUnicodeMap() which allocates a full glyph-to-unicode array and fills it via the platform font backend. On Windows this calls into DirectWrite.

DWriteFontTypeface::getGlyphToUnicodeMap calls IDWriteFontFace::GetGlyphIndices in batches via glyph_to_unicode_map(). Line 598 notes 'Intermittent DW bug on Windows 10. See crbug.com/470146.' — DirectWrite is already known to have intermittent issues. Concurrent GetGlyphIndices calls may race on internal DW state.
DWrite calls are known to not be thread-safe on Windows 8/8.1; a static mutex protects only SkScalerContext operations (glyph rendering/metrics) but not getGlyphToUnicodeMap or onCharsToGlyphs paths.

SKSvgCanvas.Create is a thin wrapper over sk_svgcanvas_create_with_stream. The C API does not expose the flags parameter (kConvertTextToPaths_Flag), so the user cannot opt into path-based text rendering as a workaround via the public API.

SkSVGCanvas::Make accepts a uint32_t flags parameter including kConvertTextToPaths_Flag (0x01) which would bypass the glyph-to-unicode path entirely by emitting text as <path> elements. This flag is not exposed in the C API.

nextQuestions: Does the race condition occur in textToGlyphs (glyph ID generation) or getGlyphToUnicodeMap (reverse mapping), or both?
Would exposing kConvertTextToPaths_Flag in the C API provide a viable workaround?
Is the DirectWrite race condition a known upstream Skia issue, or specific to our fork?
Can a mutex around GetGlyphIndices calls fix this without significant performance impact?

resolution.hypothesis: DirectWrite's IDWriteFontFace::GetGlyphIndices (or related internal state) is not fully thread-safe when called concurrently from multiple threads, even on different font face objects. This causes getGlyphToUnicodeMap to return an all-zero array intermittently, which SVGTextBuilder interprets as all-NUL characters and discards everything.

proposals.title: Serialize SVG text generation with a lock
proposals.description: Wrap SVG canvas creation and text drawing in a SemaphoreSlim(1,1) to prevent concurrent DirectWrite access. This eliminates the race condition at the cost of serializing SVG generation.

proposals.title: Expose kConvertTextToPaths_Flag in C API and C# wrapper
proposals.description: Add a flags parameter to sk_svgcanvas_create_with_stream and SKSvgCanvas.Create. When kConvertTextToPaths_Flag is set, text is emitted as <path> elements, bypassing the GlyphsToUnichars/DirectWrite code path entirely. This preserves parallelism but changes SVG output format (text is no longer selectable).

proposals.title: Add synchronization in Skia's DirectWrite glyph mapping
proposals.description: Add a mutex around the getGlyphToUnicodeMap call path in SkTypeface_win_dw.cpp to serialize DirectWrite GetGlyphIndices calls. This fixes the root cause but requires a native code change and may impact text rendering performance.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Serialize SVG text generation with a lock
resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest immediate workaround for the reporter. The lock-based approach eliminates the race condition with minimal code change. For a proper fix, exposing kConvertTextToPaths_Flag would be the best medium-term solution.

actionability.reason: Well-documented bug with complete reproduction. The root cause is likely in DirectWrite's thread safety, but needs deeper investigation to determine the exact race condition point and whether the fix should be in Skia or in SkiaSharp's C# layer.

actions.description: Labels are already correct — no changes needed
Acknowledge bug with workaround and technical analysis
